rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF KANE JACKSON LEUNG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
rulings.Rulings: Sook-Ling Leung's ("Leung") Notice to Rescind, etc., which the Court has construed as a Motion to Rescind the Mediation Agreement of July 20, 2018 ("the Agreement"), is DENIED. "There is no arbitrary right to rescind. Allegations of fraud and misrepresentation, unsupported in fact, do not form the legal basis for rescinding a contract." (Contra Costa County Title Co. v. Waloff (1960) 184 Cal.App.2d 59, 65.) Leung has failed to demonstrate that any of the statutory grounds for rescission exist or apply in this case. (See Civil Code § 1689.) The facts offered in support of her motion do not substantiate her claims that Wengai Zhao failed to disclose material information before, during, or after the mediation on July 20, 2018, or that Leung acted without "informed consent" when she signed the Agreement on July 20, 2018. Additionally, CCP § 473(b) does not apply in this case as no judgment or dismissal has been entered pursuant to the parties' Agreement. Leung's attempt to rescind the Agreement is ineffective. The Agreement stands. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must notify opposing counsel and the courtroom clerk at (415) 551-3702 by 4:00 p.m., Wednesday, September 26, 2018.
